title: BlackSky Technology (BKSY): 42% Surge in 30 Days on Major Contracts and Satellite Expansions
text: BlackSky Technology surged 42% over 30 days, moving from $27.03 to $38.44, with quarterly gains reaching 58%. The company's latest price stands at $40.51 as of May 15, 2026. Key Growth Drivers: Recent Contract Wins: On April 22, BlackSky landed a $25 million multi-year assured contract with a major international defense customer, guaranteeing 35-45 daily image collections. Additionally, the company secured a $99 million sole-source IDIQ from the U.S. government in early April. Financial Performance: Management lifted 2026 revenue guidance to $120-145 million—implying 24% growth at the midpoint, following Q4 earnings that showed revenue up 16% year-over-year to $35.2 million. Business Overview: BlackSky operates in space-based intelligence, providing real-time geospatial imagery, analytics, and high-frequency monitoring through satellite constellations. The company serves government and commercial clients with subscription-based 'assured' access to imagery through the BlackSky Spectra platform. Forward Outlook: Key catalysts include Q1 2026 earnings on May 7, new defense contracts, and expanding Gen-3 satellite utilization. The company maintains backlog over $200 million, supporting sustained growth momentum.
